quoteFirst: The smoke rose out of her chimbly this mornin’ at exactly
quoteLast: , jest an hour and two minutes earlier than it was yesterday mornin’, and half an hour and twenty seconds earlier than it was the mornin’ before that.”
title:
Josiah Allen's Wife as a P. A. and P. I.: Samantha at the Centennial.
Designed As a Bright and Shining Light, to Pierce the Fogs of Error and Injustice That Surround Society and Josiah, and to Bring More Clearly to View the Path That Leads Straight on to Virtue and Happiness.
author: Holley, Marietta, 1836-1926

quoteFirst: At 6.14 P.M. the enemy’s salvoes were falling near ships of the 1st Battle Squadron, and the _Marlborough’s_ Division of the Battle Squadron became engaged with some ships of the enemy’s Battle Fleet at
quoteLast: immediately after turning for the deployment.
title: The Grand Fleet, 1914-1916
author: Jellicoe, John Rushworth, 1859-1935

quoteFirst: The Battle Cruisers steamed some 64 miles between 3.48 P.M., the time of opening fire, and
quoteLast: the time that the Battle Fleet commenced action, and a further distance of some 57 miles to 9 P.M., when the Fleet turned to the southward for the night.
title: The Grand Fleet, 1914-1916
author: Jellicoe, John Rushworth, 1859-1935

quoteFirst: The First Battle Squadron, which was in the rear on deployment,     became engaged during deployment, the Vice-Admiral opening fire     at
quoteLast: on a battleship of the “Kaiser” class.
title: The Grand Fleet, 1914-1916
author: Jellicoe, John Rushworth, 1859-1935

quoteFirst: The action between the battle fleets lasted intermittently from
quoteLast: to 8.20 P.M. at ranges between 9,000 and 12,000 yards,     during which time the British Fleet made alterations of course from     S.E. by E. to W. in the endeavour to close.
title: The Grand Fleet, 1914-1916
author: Jellicoe, John Rushworth, 1859-1935

quoteLast: by firing seven salvoes at a ship of     the “Kaiser” class, then engaged a cruiser, and again a battleship,     and at 6.54 she was hit by a torpedo and took up a considerable     list to starboard, but re-opened at 7.3 P.M. at a cruiser and at     7.12 P.M. fired fourteen rapid salvoes at a ship of the “König”     class, hitting her frequently until she turned out of the line.
title: The Grand Fleet, 1914-1916
author: Jellicoe, John Rushworth, 1859-1935

quoteFirst: The first point essential to an understanding is that Jellicoe’s deployment was not complete until late in the afternoon,
